title: The pipeline of assembly and annotation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bfeto","text":"This protocol provides the detailed methods of assembly and annotation of the R. crenulata genome.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Filter the input raw sequences by using SOAPfilter (version 2.2). SOAP: short oligonucleotide alignment program 2.2 linux http://soap.genomics.org.cn/#down2 BGI using parameters "-y -z -p -M 2"

Estimate the genome size (420 Mb) with k-mer analysis. k=17; reads from 250 bp-insert library as input; the genome size with the formula: G = N*(L − 17 + 1)/K_depth, where N and L are the total number of reads and the length of reads, respectively, and K_depth indicates the frequency of k-mers occurring more frequently than the others.

Run Platanus (version 1.2.4 ) to assemble our genome. Platanus Assembler (PLATform for Assembling NUcleotide Sequences 1.2.4 linux http://platanus.bio.titech.ac.jp/platanus/?page_id=14 Tokyo Institute of Technology performing "assemble (-k 35 -m 130 -u 0.2 -c 10 -s 10 -t 32)->scaffold (-l 3 -u 0.2 -v 32 -s 32)->gap_close (-t 32 -s 32 -ed 0.1)" modes in turn; assemble (-k 35 -m 130 -u 0.2 -c 10 -s 10 -t 32)scaffold (-l 3 -u 0.2 -v 32 -s 32)gap_close (-t 32 -s 32 -ed 0.1) (Perform assemble -> scaffold -> gap_close modes in turn) assemble (-k 35 -m 130 -u 0.2 -c 10 -s 10 -t 32) scaffold (-l 3 -u 0.2 -v 32 -s 32) gap_close (-t 32 -s 32 -ed 0.1) (Perform assemble -> scaffold -> gap_close modes in turn)
Perform Gapcloser (version 1.10) to further close gaps in our genome obtained in step3. GapCloser 1.10 linux http://soap.genomics.org.cn/soapdenovo.html BGI using reads from all insert-size libraries
Run RepeatModeler, RepeatScout and LTR_FINDER, respectively, to build de novo library based on the input assembled genome sequence. version 1.0.5

Based on the library constructed above as database, run RepeatMasker (version 3.3.0) to find and then classify the repetitive sequences. RepeatMasker 3.3.0 linux Institute for Systems Biology using parameters "-nolow -no_is -norna -parallel 1"

Run RepeatMasker and RepeatProteinMask (version 3.3.0) to identify repeats in the genome at DNA and protein level, respectively, by aligning sequences against existing databases, Repbase TE library (Version 17.01) and TE protein database. using parameters "-noLowSimple -pvalue 0.0001" when running RepeatProteinMask
Mask these repetitive regions obtained above (step 5-7) with 'N's.

Run Augustus (version 2.5.5) and GlimmerHMM (version 3.0.1) to de novo predict genes in the repeat-masked genome sequences. using parameters "--species=arabidopsis --uniqueGeneId=true --noInFrameStop=true --gff3=on --strand=both" when running Augustus; using parameters "-f -g" and trained by arabidopsis when running GlimmerHMM

Download protein sequences of homlog species (arabidopsis, wild strawberry, peach and Chinese plum), then align these against our masked genome sequences with BLAT, and then based on the BLAT mapping results, run GeneWise (version 2.2.0 ) to predict genes. with parameters "--max divergence rate 0.3 --extend length for both sides of regions 2000"
Integrate genes predicted in step 9-10 to obtain the consensus gene set by using GLEAN. filtering with criterion "overlap cutoff 0.8 and at least one homolog support"

Perform TopHat (version 2.1.0) with default parameters to align clean RNA-seq reads against gene set mentioned in Step11, and then use Cufflinks (version 2.2.1) to assemble these transcripts, then use training parameters to predict ORFs, and finally obtain the more intergrity and trusty gene set. filtering RNA sequencing data firstly by performing SOAPnuke with parameters “-l 10 -q 0.5 -n 0.01 -f AGTCGGAGGCCAAGCGGTCTTAGGAAGACAA -Q 2”
Run BUSCO and map transcriptome data to our final gene set to assess the completeness.

Map protein sequences of the final gene set to existing databases to identify their functions or motifs, such as SwissProt, TrEMBL, KEGG, InterPro. SwissProt, TrEMBL and KEGG: using BLASTP; Interpro: using InterProScan (version 4.7) with seven different models (Profilescan, blastprodom, HmmSmart, HmmPanther, HmmPfam, FPrintScan and Pattern-Scan)
creator: Gigascience Database
